what_it_does: Defines global-script React subcomponents and helpers for TypeBuilder: a registry-backed type browser, draft preview, layer archetype renderer, inspector forms, JSON/default/tag editors, review panel, and slot-acceptance modal, then exposes them on window.
observations: Uses aliased React hooks from the global React object rather than module imports.
Reads registry and renderer services from window.CV_REGISTRY, window.TypeThumb, and window.WS_BLOCKS.
BuilderPreview resolves a draft against an optional parent type and merges slots, defaults, variables, and tags for display.
Vi mode collects a natural-language brief and delegates schema drafting through callback props.
Slot acceptance is edited through layers, families, and tags, with registry candidate preview.
Several text editors keep local raw text state and commit parsed values on blur.
standouts: Layer-specific archetype rendering covers token, atom, block, system, surface, and doc types.
The inspector combines structured editing with a JSON editor escape path.
Slot compatibility is treated as an inspectable design constraint rather than only a stored field.
conventions: Global-script React style with window-level dependencies and exports.
Components receive draft mutation callbacks rather than owning persistent data.
Patch callbacks are mostly passed partial objects; JsonEditor passes a parsed whole draft object.
Structured text inputs parse and patch on blur.
Visual sections use tb-* class naming and existing dsa-* button classes.
concerns: BuilderLibrary computes a types value and receives a patch prop, but neither is used in the rendered output or callbacks.
DefaultsEditor, TagsField, and JsonEditor reset their raw editor text only when draft.id changes, so same-id external draft changes may not appear in the textarea state.
BuilderPreview memoizes resolved data only on draft, so registry parent changes alone would not recompute the resolved preview.
JsonEditor calls patch(next) with a whole parsed object while other editors use partial patches, which depends on patch semantics not shown here.
notes: This is the file-local UI layer for authoring Type definitions through browse, preview, form, JSON, Vi-assisted, and slot-compatibility workflows.
questions: Does window.CV_REGISTRY.subscribe return a React effect cleanup function?
Is patch intended to merge partials and also accept a whole draft object?
Should raw editor state update when the same draft id receives changes from another editor path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches: the file contains TypeBuilder subcomponents and helper functions split out from the larger TypeBuilder surface.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make design-system Type authoring editable through multiple representations: searchable registry, visual preview, structured forms, raw JSON, and assisted proposal.
novelty: It combines layer-aware design substrate previews with an editable slot acceptance model and a registry candidate preview inside the same authoring surface.
contribution: Contributes an authoring UI vocabulary for design substrates: registry browsing, inheritance-aware draft rendering, layer archetypes, schema/default/variable/tag editing, and slot compatibility selection.
summary_for_embedding: Global React components and helpers for TypeBuilder authoring, including library navigation, preview resolution, archetype rendering, inspector forms, JSON editors, and slot-acceptance filtering.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for editing and previewing design-system Type definitions across visual, form, JSON, and Vi-assisted workflows.

symbols.does: Renders the left rail type browser and dispatches open/new-type events.
symbols.description: This component subscribes to the registry, queries it with the current browse filter, renders filter/tree controls, and emits tb-open CustomEvents for switching to an existing type or starting a blank type. It asks for confirmation before replacing the current draft with another selected type.

symbols.does: Renders either the Vi drafting panel or an inheritance-aware live preview of the draft type.
symbols.description: This component resolves a draft against its parent type when draft.extends is set, merging slots, defaults, variables, and tags for preview purposes. In non-Vi mode it shows metadata, lineage, description, visual thumbnail, slots, defaults, and variables; in Vi mode it collects a brief and calls viPropose.

symbols.does: Renders a representative preview for a resolved type based on its layer.
symbols.description: This component branches on draft.layer and produces layer-specific markup for token, atom, block, system, surface, and doc types. It uses defaults, icon metadata, runtime block renderers, slots, and labels to show what the type looks like in context.

symbols.does: Displays default schema fields as key/value rows or an empty-state hint.
symbols.description: This helper component turns the defaults object into a compact schema preview. When no default keys exist, it shows guidance that Vi can propose a schema in the Vi tab.

symbols.does: Formats a field value into a short display string.
symbols.description: This utility returns an em dash for nullish values, raw strings for strings, String() output for numbers and booleans, and a truncated JSON representation for other values. It is used by SchemaPreview for readable schema rows.

symbols.does: Merges two variable arrays by variable key with later values overriding earlier ones.
symbols.description: This utility stores variables in a Map keyed by v.key. Parent variables are inserted first and child variables merge over them, preserving one variable object per key in the returned array.

symbols.does: Renders the right rail inspector or delegates to the JSON editor based on mode.
symbols.description: This component switches to JsonEditor when mode is json. Otherwise it renders sections for basics, lineage, slots, default data, variables, and tags, wiring each section to the relevant patch and add/remove callbacks.

symbols.does: Renders editable basic fields for a draft type.
symbols.description: This form edits name, stable id, description, layer, family, and icon. The layer selector uses window.CV_REGISTRY layer metadata for option labels.

symbols.does: Renders the parent type selector for draft inheritance.
symbols.description: This form lets the user choose no parent or one of the provided parentCandidates as draft.extends. When a parent is selected, it displays a hint that slots, defaults, and variables are inherited and can be overridden.

symbols.does: Renders a compact Vi prompt form for drafting a type from a brief.
symbols.description: This component contains a textarea for describing the type and a Vi-styled button that calls viPropose. The button is disabled while busy or when the brief is blank.

symbols.does: Renders editable cards for the draft's slots.
symbols.description: This component lists slot keys from draft.slots, allows label editing, multiple and optional toggles, removal, and opening the slot browser for accepted-type constraints. It shows an empty-state hint when no slots exist.

symbols.does: Provides a JSON textarea for editing draft.defaults.
symbols.description: This editor stores raw JSON text locally, initializes it from draft.defaults, and commits parsed JSON to patch({ defaults: v }) on blur. Parse failures are displayed as local error text.

symbols.does: Parses DefaultsEditor raw JSON and patches draft.defaults.
symbols.description: This nested function attempts to parse raw as JSON, applies the parsed object through patch({ defaults: v }), and clears parse errors. If parsing fails, it stores the exception message for display.

symbols.does: Renders editable rows for draft variable definitions.
symbols.description: This component edits each variable's key, label, default value, and kind through patchVariable. It also supports removing variables and shows a hint when the variable list is empty.

symbols.does: Edits draft tags through a comma-separated input.
symbols.description: This component mirrors draft.tags into a local comma-separated string and commits a trimmed, de-duplicated tag array on blur. It resets its local input when draft.id changes.

symbols.does: Parses TagsField comma-separated text and patches draft.tags.
symbols.description: This nested function splits the raw tag string by comma, trims entries, removes empty strings, de-duplicates via Set, and sends the resulting array through patch.

symbols.does: Provides a full-draft JSON textarea editor.
symbols.description: This editor serializes the entire draft into local raw text, lets the user edit it, and attempts to parse and patch the parsed object on blur. Parse errors are kept in component state and rendered below the textarea.

symbols.does: Parses JsonEditor raw JSON and patches the parsed draft object.
symbols.description: This nested function attempts JSON.parse(raw), passes the resulting object to patch, and clears the local error. On parse failure it records the error message for the JSON editor UI.

symbols.does: Displays draft name, description, and formatted JSON.
symbols.description: This component renders a simple review view with the draft title, muted description, and a preformatted JSON serialization of the draft object.

symbols.does: Renders a modal for configuring what a slot accepts.
symbols.description: This modal keeps local acceptance state for layers, families, and tags, previews matching registry candidates with candidatesForSlot, and applies the configured accepts object through onApply. It also supports closing via backdrop, close button, or cancel.

symbols.does: Toggles a layer inside SlotBrowserModal acceptance state.
symbols.description: This nested function updates acc.layers by removing the selected layer if present or appending it if absent. It preserves the rest of the acceptance state.

symbols.does: Parses SlotBrowserModal family input into acc.families.
symbols.description: This nested function splits the familyInput string by commas, trims each value, removes empty values, and writes the resulting array into acceptance state.

symbols.does: Parses SlotBrowserModal tag input into acc.tags.
symbols.description: This nested function splits the tagInput string by commas, trims each value, removes empty values, and writes the resulting array into acceptance state.
